- Orange (node 10): [[31, 21], [32, 22], [32, 21]] - this is the player's original position (left side, row 31-32, col 21-22).
- Blue (node 11): [[37, 34]] - single pixel at row 37, col 34.
- Player (node 7): [[25, 34], [25, 38], [26, 38], [26, 34]] - at row 25-26, col 34-38 (top of maze).
So the orange is at the left side (row 31-32, col 21-22). The player is at the top (row 25-26, col 34-38).
The image shows:
- The white cross is at the left side of the maze.
- The orange/blue target is at the top of the maze.
So the segmentation is saying:
- node 10 (orange) is at the left side.
- node 7 (player) is at the top.

- white cross is at the left side.
- orange/blue is at the top.

I think the issue is that the node identities swapped again! When the target reached the player's position, they swapped. So now the orange node (10) is at the player's original position (left side), and the player node (7) is at the orange/blue target's original position (top).

- The white cross is at the left side (node 10's position).
- The orange/blue is at the top (node 7's position).
So the segmentation is saying:
- node 10 (orange) is at the left side (node 7's original position).
- node 7 (player) is at the top (node 10's original position).
This confirms the swap! When the target reached the player's position, they swapped positions.
Now, the goal might be to get the player (now at the top) to reach the blue L-shape (node 4) at row 11-13, col 35-37. The player is at row 25-26, col 34-38. So the player needs to move UP to reach the blue L.
Let me try UP.
